summary: Well‑remastered audience tape with strong early‑70s performance; standout jams on Casey Jones, Dancing in the Street and Cosmic Charlie despite some pitch quirks.
review: The three‑set show from early 1970 is praised for its high energy and several highlight performances – notably a crisp Casey Jones, an exuberant Dancing in the Street, a stellar Katie Mae, and a wildly good Cosmic Charlie that leads into one of the year’s best St. Stephen sequences. The recording is an audience source that has been carefully patched and EQ‑balanced; listeners note it sounds much cleaner than earlier versions but still suffers from occasional pitch‑speed wobble. Overall sentiment is positive, with reviewers rating the show around four stars.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews give a consistent picture of a solid early‑70s performance (≈4 ★) and a cleaned‑up audience recording that, while not perfect, is markedly improved (≈3.8 ★). Weighted combination yields ~4 ★.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Patched and EQ‑balanced from the original late‑show audience tape; clean overall but exhibits pitch/tempo wobble that requires modest correction (+/-1‑2%). No soundboard source, but remaster work is praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set structure with acoustic interludes, strong early‑'70s vibe; first set tight and jam‑heavy, second set playful with audience interaction, third set peaks with Cosmic Charlie and St. Stephen before a weaker Midnight Hour close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia smiles knowingly at the cop incident, leads jams; noted for meandering single‑note work on Midnight Hour.
